news.headline: BNB Chain debuts 'BNB My Valentine' campaign with NFT rewards and prizes, celebrating Valentine's Day with global crypto lovers.

news.headline: Gabelli Healthcare & WellnessRx Trust (NYSE: GRX) declares $0.15 per share cash distribution for Q1, payable on March 24th.

news.headline: U.S. Producer Price Index rose 3.5% year over year in January, beating expectations with a monthly increase of 0.4%. Core PPI climbed 3.6% annually, with a monthly increase of 0.3%. Fed is still monitoring PCE index for inflation outcomes. Fed Chair Powell suggested waiting for a full picture before drawing conclusions.
news.summary: U.S. producer prices rose more than anticipated in January, with the Producer Price Index surging 3.5% year over year, the highest increase since February 2023. This exceeded economists' expectations of a 3.2% increase and marked an acceleration from December's 3.3%. The core PPI, excluding food and energy, climbed 3.6% annually and 0.3% monthly, surpassing forecasts. Despite these numbers raising concerns, some components crucial for Fed's inflation gauge like the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index showed softer readings. Fed Chair Powell indicated that it's too early to draw conclusions before a comprehensive view. The PCE is estimated to have a 0.4% increase and a 2.5% year-over-year rise in the inflation metric targeted by the Fed. The full picture is awaited before making concrete assessments.

news.headline: India's loyalty programs market set to reach $3.58 billion by 2025, growing at 18.3% annually with CAGR of 20.0%.
news.summary: A new report titled "India Loyalty Programs Market Intelligence and Future Growth Dynamics" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The loyalty market in India is projected to increase by 18.3% annually, reaching US$3.58 billion by 2025. Throughout the past years, the loyalty market in India has shown a consistent growth, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 20.0%. The report provides over 50 Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) on loyalty program trends in various sectors, operational KPIs, retail product dynamics, and consumer demographics.

news.headline: Evidently hires Kai Romero, MD, as head of clinical success after closing $15M Series A funding.

news.headline: Passenger sues American Airlines for negligence after two emergency landings, citing dangerous condition on Boeing plane.

news.headline: Sandvine achieves approval for restructuring on Jan 30, 2025, to emerge from CCAA protection through sale of operations.
news.summary: Sandvine Corporation, based in Plano, Texas, has recently obtained official approval from the Ontario Superior Court of Justice (Commercial List) on January 30, 2025, for its proposed restructuring plan. This approval paves the way for the company to exit the protection provided by the Companies’ Creditors Arrangement Act ("CCAA") through a planned sale of its operations. The restructuring will involve transferring its operations to a group of well-funded newly established entities.

news.headline: Mullen Automotive Inc. to undergo 1-for-60 reverse stock split effective Feb. 18, 2025, with new CUSIP number 62526P604.
news.summary: BREA, California - On February 13, 2025, Mullen Automotive Inc. (NASDAQ: MULN), a leading electric vehicle manufacturer, disclosed plans for a 1-for-60 reverse stock split of its common stock, valued at $0.001 per share. This initiative is set to take effect on February 18, 2025, at 12:01 a.m. Eastern Time. The company's common stock will remain listed on The Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol MULN and will commence trading on a split-adjusted basis upon the market's opening on the same day. Post the Reverse Stock Split, the new CUSIP number for the Common Stock will be 62526P604.

Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ:PANW) to disclose Q2 earnings, emphasize platformization strategy. CEO optimistic about its impact on security and AI.
news.summary: Palo Alto Networks, Inc. (NASDAQ:PANW) is scheduled to release its second-quarter earnings post Thursday's market close. Investors are keen on updates regarding the company's transition from a focus on single-product delivery to its "platformization" strategy. CEO Nikesh Arora expressed confidence in this shift following the first-quarter results. He emphasized that the growing market acceptance of platformization as a significant solution for security and improved AI outcomes is a game-changer. Arora predicts this trend will persist for several years, with Palo Alto Networks well-positioned to cater to customer needs. For further details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Bitcoin faces US PPI impact with warnings of lower position; crypto struggles in market.
news.summary: In recent statistics, it has been reported that the number of COVID-19 cases in the United States has surpassed 30 million, with over 550,000 deaths recorded. This surge in cases has prompted a nationwide effort to increase vaccine distribution and administration. Additionally, the global death toll from the pandemic has now reached over 2.5 million. news.headline: Wall Street banks optimistic about Trump's deregulation agenda. Analyst notes positive outlook from 30+ banks at the Bank of America conference. Looser regulations could lead to increased growth opportunities. Trade war concerns remain.
news.summary: Wall Street banks appear undeterred by the administration’s tariff policy, as per Bank of America analyst Ebrahim H. Poonawala. Poonawala highlighted the optimism among Wall Street banks at the recent Bank of America financial services conference. Executives from more than 30 banks shared the sentiment that relaxed regulations would allow financial teams to focus on growth opportunities rather than compliance issues. The insight was gathered from discussions at BofA’s 33rd annual financial services conference. For more details, refer to the complete story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Validic launches AI-powered remote patient monitoring tool using Generative AI, aiding healthcare providers to analyze patient data trends.
news.summary: A healthcare technology innovation company, Validic, based in Durham, North Carolina, has introduced a new patent-pending solution: a digital remote patient monitoring (RPM) assistant that utilizes Generative AI (GenAI) technology. The cutting-edge technology aims to enhance healthcare providers' understanding of patient health, improving the quality of care delivered.
